body: {"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative with subsystem + change named. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with four concrete file:line references (workers.go:25-49, agent_cmd.go:1582-1605, server.go:2395-2409, graphql_adapters.go:63-75), a detailed PROPOSED FIX naming every field and method, and NON-SCOPE. AC is numbered, each criterion is verifiable, two named Test* functions are present (TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTrip_AllFields, TestExecuteLoopSpec_ApplyDefaults), and the final two lines are exactly the required `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpath\u003e/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes` gate. Labels cover phase, area (dual), kind, and prevention. Parent is set. One gap: the bead body never declares 'no deps' or lists dependencies — the rubric requires an explicit statement either way, and a cold-reading agent cannot determine whether any blocking beads exist. Notes field references 'fresh-eyes cleanup' as prior-session context, but since this is informational rather than load-bearing for execution it does not fail the standalone criterion.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Add an explicit 'Dependencies: none' (or list actual dep IDs) to the description to satisfy the parent+deps rubric criterion."],"waivers_applied":[]}

Push was blocked by three test failures unrelated to the new package: (1) TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook in cmd/ expected model="smart" but got "", which is outside the bead's scope (bead explicitly forbids modifying cobra/cmd callers); (2) TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer and (3) TestRuntimelintCleanTree both fail with 'error obtaining VCS status: exit status 128' — a known infra artifact of go build running inside a detached tmp worktree. Operator should confirm whether TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook is a pre-existing regression on main (run cd cli && go test -run TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook ./cmd/... on clean main) and whether the VCS-stamp failures are an existing known-flaky pattern in the worktree harness.
